leed.name: LEED
leed.fullName: Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design
leed.description: The world's most widely used green building rating system. Covers design, construction, operations and maintenance. Projects earn points across categories including energy, water, materials and indoor quality, achieving Certified, Silver, Gold or Platinum levels.

breeam.name: BREEAM
breeam.fullName: Building Research Establishment Environmental Assessment Method
breeam.description: The world's first green building rating system, established in 1990. Assesses buildings across 10 categories including energy, health, transport, water and waste. Dominant in the UK and widely adopted across Europe and the Middle East.

passivhaus.name: Passivhaus
passivhaus.description: A rigorous voluntary standard for ultra-low energy buildings. Requires heating demand ≤15 kWh/m²/year and airtightness ≤0.6 ACH at 50 Pa. Focuses on superinsulation, thermal-bridge-free design, high-performance windows and heat recovery ventilation.

dgnb.name: DGNB
dgnb.description: Germany's holistic sustainability certification assessing buildings across environmental, economic, sociocultural, technical, process and site quality. Uses lifecycle assessment (LCA) methodology. Awards Platinum, Gold, Silver or Bronze ratings.

hqe.name: HQE
hqe.description: France's leading green building certification, covering energy, environment, health and comfort. Evaluates buildings across 14 targets in 4 themes. Dominant in France and French-speaking countries with over 620,000 certified buildings.

minergie.name: Minergie
minergie.description: Switzerland's energy performance standard for new and renovated buildings. Three tiers: Minergie (base), Minergie-P (passive-level), and Minergie-A (net-zero). Over 61,000 certified buildings, mainly in Switzerland and Liechtenstein.

estidama.name: Estidama
estidama.description: Abu Dhabi's sustainability framework rating buildings from 1 to 5 Pearls across energy, water, materials, natural systems and livability. Mandatory for all new construction in the emirate — 1 Pearl minimum for private, 2 Pearls for government projects.

casbee.name: CASBEE
casbee.fullName: Comprehensive Assessment System for Built Environment Efficiency
casbee.description: Japan's comprehensive green building assessment tool. Evaluates the ratio of building environmental quality (Q) to environmental load (L), producing a BEE index. Rates buildings from C (poor) to S (superior). Used for both new and existing buildings.

nabers.name: NABERS
nabers.fullName: National Australian Built Environment Rating System
nabers.description: Australia's national performance-based rating system measuring actual energy, water, waste and indoor environment quality of operating buildings. Rates from 1 to 6 stars. Mandatory disclosure for commercial offices over 1,000 m² since 2010.

three_star.name: Three Star
three_star.description: China's national green building rating system administered by MOHURD. Evaluates buildings across land use, energy, water, materials, indoor environment and operations. Three Star (★★★) is the highest tier, awarded to the most resource-efficient projects.
three_star.organization: Ministry of Housing and Urban-Rural Development (MOHURD)

living_building_challenge.name: Living Building Challenge
living_building_challenge.description: The world's most rigorous green building standard. Requires net-positive energy and water, zero carbon, responsible materials and biophilic design — all verified over 12 months of actual operation. Fewer than 200 buildings certified globally.

nearly_zero_energy_buildings.name: NZEB
nearly_zero_energy_buildings.fullName: Nearly Zero Energy Buildings (EU/EEA only, EPBD mandate since 2021)
nearly_zero_energy_buildings.description: EU-mandated building energy standard under the Energy Performance of Buildings Directive (EPBD). Since 2021, all new buildings in the EU/EEA must be nearly zero-energy, with very low energy demand largely covered by renewable sources.

local_energy_label.name: Local Energy Label
local_energy_label.fullName: National/Local Energy Performance Certificate or Rating
local_energy_label.description: Country-specific energy performance certificates or ratings issued by national authorities. Requirements and stringency vary widely — from mandatory EPCs in EU states to voluntary schemes in other regions. Not directly comparable across countries.
